    1. loadFromCsv(String, String)

Class HotelDataLoader

java.lang.Object
pl.edu.agh.kis.pz1.io.HotelDataLoader
public class HotelDataLoader extends Object
Utility class responsible for loading hotel configuration and state from CSV files.

Behavioral notes: - The first line of the CSV is treated as a header and skipped. - Each subsequent line is split by comma (with split(",", -1) to preserve empty fields). - Expected columns (by index): roomNumber, description, price, capacity, guests, checkinDate, notes. - If the guests field is non-empty, guests are parsed (pipe-delimited), check-in date is parsed (or set to now if blank), and Room.checkin(List, LocalDate, int, String) is called with the parsed data. - On any I/O or parsing error the method logs the exception and returns a Hotel instance (possibly empty) instead of throwing.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type

    static Hotel
    loadFromCsv(String filePath, String hotelName)
    Load hotel configuration and state from a CSV file.

  • Method Details

    • loadFromCsv

      public static Hotel loadFromCsv(String filePath, String hotelName)
      Load hotel configuration and state from a CSV file.

      The method constructs a Hotel named hotelName and populates it with rooms parsed from the file. If a guests field is present and non-empty, the room will be marked as occupied via Room.checkin(List, LocalDate, int, String); note that this loader currently passes a fixed 3 as the days parameter to checkin.

      On any exception (I/O or parsing) the method logs the problem and returns the Hotel instance constructed so far (may be empty).

      Parameters:
      filePath - path to the CSV file
      hotelName - name for the created Hotel instance
      Returns:
      populated Hotel instance (never null)
